tidyvegan 0.1.0
  • Reference
    • Vegan FAQ
  • Changelog

Diagrams and Procedures for Partition of Variation

Pierre Legendre

processed with vegan 0.1.0 in R version 4.6.1 (2026-06-24) on 2026-06-30

Source: vignettes/partitioning.Rmd
partitioning.Rmd
## Loading required package: permute

Diagrams describing the partitions of variation of a response data table by two (Figure 1), three (Figure 2) and four tables (Figure 3) of explanatory variables. The fraction names [a] to [p] in the output of the varpart function follow the notation in these Venn diagrams, and the diagrams were produced using the showvarparts function.

Figure 1: Partitioning with 2 Tables

3 regression/ canonical analyses and 3 subtraction equations are needed to estimate the 4 (=2^2) fractions. [a] and [b] can be tested for significance (3 canonical analyses per permutation). Fraction [c] cannot be tested singly.

Figure 2: Partitioning with 3 Tables 

#> Warning in pt - sw: longer object length is not a multiple of shorter object
#> length
#> Warning in pt + sw: longer object length is not a multiple of shorter object
#> length
#> labels do not fit the space, switching to X1...

7 regression/ canonical analyses and 10 subtraction equations are needed to estimate the 8 (=2^3) fractions. [a] to [c] and subsets containing [a] to [c] can be tested for significance (4 canonical analyses per permutation to test [a] to [c]). Fractions [d] to [g] cannot be tested singly.

Figure 3: Partitioning with 4 Tables 

#> labels do not fit the space, switching to X1...

15 regression/ canonical analyses and 27 subtraction equations are needed to estimate the 16 (=2^4) fractions. [a] to [d] and subsets containing [a] to [d] can be tested for significance (5 canonical analyses per permutation to test [a] to [d]). Fractions [e] to [o] cannot be tested singly.

Procedures for Variation Partitioning

Below are the detailed algebraic procedures, derived fractions, and degrees of freedom for partitioning variation with two, three, and four explanatory tables.

1. Two Explanatory Data Tables

  • Table 1 with m1m_1 explanatory variables.
  • Table 2 with m2m_2 explanatory variables.
  • Number of fractions: 4, called [a][a] to [d][d].
  • ✓\checkmark indicates the 3 regression or canonical analyses that have to be computed.
  • #\# indicates partial canonical analyses (only computed if tests of significance or biplots are needed).
Type Compute Fitted Residuals Derived fractions Degrees of freedom
✓\checkmark Y⋅1Y \cdot 1 [a+b][a+b] [c+d][c+d] (1) df(a+b)=m1\text{df}(a+b) = m_1
✓\checkmark Y⋅2Y \cdot 2 [b+c][b+c] [a+d][a+d] (2) df(b+c)=m2\text{df}(b+c) = m_2
✓\checkmark Y⋅1,2Y \cdot 1,2 [a+b+c][a+b+c] [d][d] (3) df(a+b+c)=m3≤m1+m2\text{df}(a+b+c) = m_3 \le m_1+m_2
#\# Y⋅1|2Y \cdot 1 \vert 2 [a][a] [d][d] df(a)=m3−m2\text{df}(a) = m_3-m_2
#\# Y⋅2|1Y \cdot 2 \vert 1 [c][c] [d][d] df(c)=m3−m1\text{df}(c) = m_3-m_1

Partial Analyses

  • Controlling for 1 table XX:
      1. [a]=[a+b+c]−[b+c][a] = [a+b+c] - [b+c]   df(a)=m3−m2*\text{df}(a) = m_3-m_2^*
      1. [c]=[a+b+c]−[a+b][c] = [a+b+c] - [a+b]   df(c)=m3−m1*\text{df}(c) = m_3-m_1^*
      1. [b]=[a+b]+[b+c]−[a+b+c][b] = [a+b] + [b+c] - [a+b+c]   df(b)=m1+m2−(m1+m2)=0\text{df}(b) = m_1+m_2-(m_1+m_2) = 0
      1. [d]=residuals=1−[a+b+c][d] = \text{residuals} = 1 - [a+b+c]   df2(d)=n−1−m3\text{df}_2(d) = n-1-m_3 (for denominator of FF)

* Calculation of d.f. for difference between nested models: see Sokal & Rohlf (1981, 1995) equation 16.14.

Tests of Significance

  • F(a+b)=[a+b]/m1[c+d]/(n−1−m1)F(a+b) = \frac{[a+b]/m_1}{[c+d]/(n-1-m_1)}
  • F(b+c)=[b+c]/m2[a+d]/(n−1−m2)F(b+c) = \frac{[b+c]/m_2}{[a+d]/(n-1-m_2)}
  • F(a+b+c)=[a+b+c]/m3[d]/(n−1−m3)F(a+b+c) = \frac{[a+b+c]/m_3}{[d]/(n-1-m_3)}
  • F(a)=[a]/(m3−m2)[d]/(n−1−m3)F(a) = \frac{[a]/(m_3-m_2)}{[d]/(n-1-m_3)}
  • F(c)=[c]/(m3−m1)[d]/(n−1−m3)F(c) = \frac{[c]/(m_3-m_1)}{[d]/(n-1-m_3)}

[!NOTE] The only testable fractions are those that can be obtained directly by regression or canonical analysis. The non-testable fraction is [b][b]; that fraction cannot be obtained directly by regression or canonical analysis.

2. Three Explanatory Data Tables

  • Table 1 with m1m_1 variables, Table 2 with m2m_2 variables, Table 3 with m3m_3 variables.
  • Number of fractions: 8, called [a][a] to [h][h].
  • ✓\checkmark indicates the 7 regression or canonical analyses that have to be computed.
  • #\# indicates partial canonical analyses (only computed if tests of significance or biplots are needed).
Type Compute Fitted Residuals Derived fractions Degrees of freedom
✓\checkmark Y⋅1Y \cdot 1 [a+d+f+g][a+d+f+g] [b+c+e+h][b+c+e+h] (1) df(a+d+f+g)=m1\text{df}(a+d+f+g) = m_1
✓\checkmark Y⋅2Y \cdot 2 [b+d+e+g][b+d+e+g] [a+c+f+h][a+c+f+h] (2) df(b+d+e+g)=m2\text{df}(b+d+e+g) = m_2
✓\checkmark Y⋅3Y \cdot 3 [c+e+f+g][c+e+f+g] [a+b+d+h][a+b+d+h] (3) df(c+e+f+g)=m3\text{df}(c+e+f+g) = m_3
✓\checkmark Y⋅1,2Y \cdot 1,2 [a+b+d+e+f+g][a+b+d+e+f+g] [c+h][c+h] (4) df(a+b+d+e+f+g)=m4≤m1+m2\text{df}(a+b+d+e+f+g) = m_4 \le m_1+m_2
✓\checkmark Y⋅1,3Y \cdot 1,3 [a+c+d+e+f+g][a+c+d+e+f+g] [b+h][b+h] (5) df(a+c+d+e+f+g)=m5≤m1+m3\text{df}(a+c+d+e+f+g) = m_5 \le m_1+m_3
✓\checkmark Y⋅2,3Y \cdot 2,3 [b+c+d+e+f+g][b+c+d+e+f+g] [a+h][a+h] (6) df(b+c+d+e+f+g)=m6≤m2+m3\text{df}(b+c+d+e+f+g) = m_6 \le m_2+m_3
✓\checkmark Y⋅1,2,3Y \cdot 1,2,3 [a+b+c+d+e+f+g][a+b+c+d+e+f+g] [h][h] (7) df(a+b+c+d+e+f+g)=m7≤m1+m2+m3\text{df}(a+b+c+d+e+f+g) = m_7 \le m_1+m_2+m_3
#\# Y⋅1|2Y \cdot 1 \vert 2 [a+f][a+f] [c+h][c+h] df(a+f)=m4−m2\text{df}(a+f) = m_4-m_2
#\# Y⋅1|3Y \cdot 1 \vert 3 [a+d][a+d] [b+h][b+h] df(a+d)=m5−m3\text{df}(a+d) = m_5-m_3
#\# Y⋅2|1Y \cdot 2 \vert 1 [b+e][b+e] [c+h][c+h] df(b+e)=m4−m1\text{df}(b+e) = m_4-m_1
#\# Y⋅2|3Y \cdot 2 \vert 3 [b+d][b+d] [a+h][a+h] df(b+d)=m6−m3\text{df}(b+d) = m_6-m_3
#\# Y⋅3|1Y \cdot 3 \vert 1 [c+e][c+e] [b+h][b+h] df(c+e)=m5−m1\text{df}(c+e) = m_5-m_1
#\# Y⋅3|2Y \cdot 3 \vert 2 [c+f][c+f] [a+h][a+h] df(c+f)=m6−m2\text{df}(c+f) = m_6-m_2
#\# Y⋅1|2,3Y \cdot 1 \vert 2,3 [a][a] [h][h] df(a)=m7−m6\text{df}(a) = m_7-m_6
#\# Y⋅2|1,3Y \cdot 2 \vert 1,3 [b][b] [h][h] df(b)=m7−m5\text{df}(b) = m_7-m_5
#\# Y⋅3|1,2Y \cdot 3 \vert 1,2 [c][c] [h][h] df(c)=m7−m4\text{df}(c) = m_7-m_4

Partial Analyses

  • Controlling for two tables XX:
      1. [a]=[a+b+c+d+e+f+g]−[b+c+d+e+f+g][a] = [a+b+c+d+e+f+g] - [b+c+d+e+f+g]   df(a)=m7−m6\text{df}(a) = m_7-m_6
      1. [b]=[a+b+c+d+e+f+g]−[a+c+d+e+f+g][b] = [a+b+c+d+e+f+g] - [a+c+d+e+f+g]   df(b)=m7−m5\text{df}(b) = m_7-m_5
      1. [c]=[a+b+c+d+e+f+g]−[a+b+d+e+f+g][c] = [a+b+c+d+e+f+g] - [a+b+d+e+f+g]   df(c)=m7−m4\text{df}(c) = m_7-m_4
  • Controlling for one table XX:
      1. [a+d]=[a+c+d+e+f+g]−[c+e+f+g][a+d] = [a+c+d+e+f+g] - [c+e+f+g]   df(a+d)=m5−m3\text{df}(a+d) = m_5-m_3
      1. [a+f]=[a+b+d+e+f+g]−[b+d+e+g][a+f] = [a+b+d+e+f+g] - [b+d+e+g]   df(a+f)=m4−m2\text{df}(a+f) = m_4-m_2
      1. [b+d]=[b+c+d+e+f+g]−[c+e+f+g][b+d] = [b+c+d+e+f+g] - [c+e+f+g]   df(b+d)=m6−m3\text{df}(b+d) = m_6-m_3
      1. [b+e]=[a+b+d+e+f+g]−[a+d+f+g][b+e] = [a+b+d+e+f+g] - [a+d+f+g]   df(b+e)=m4−m1\text{df}(b+e) = m_4-m_1
      1. [c+e]=[a+c+d+e+f+g]−[a+d+f+g][c+e] = [a+c+d+e+f+g] - [a+d+f+g]   df(c+e)=m5−m1\text{df}(c+e) = m_5-m_1
      1. [c+f]=[b+c+d+e+f+g]−[b+d+e+g][c+f] = [b+c+d+e+f+g] - [b+d+e+g]   df(c+f)=m6−m2\text{df}(c+f) = m_6-m_2

Fractions Estimated by Subtraction (cannot be tested)

    1. [d]=[a+d]−[a][d] = [a+d] - [a]   df(d)=m1−m1=0\text{df}(d) = m_1-m_1 = 0
    1. [e]=[b+e]−[b][e] = [b+e] - [b]   df(e)=m2−m2=0\text{df}(e) = m_2-m_2 = 0
    1. [f]=[c+f]−[c][f] = [c+f] - [c]   df(f)=m3−m3=0\text{df}(f) = m_3-m_3 = 0
    1. [g]=[a+b+c+d+e+f+g]−[a+d]−[b+e]−[c+f][g] = [a+b+c+d+e+f+g] - [a+d] - [b+e] - [c+f]   df(g)=(m1+m2+m3)−m1−m2−m3=0\text{df}(g) = (m_1+m_2+m_3)-m_1-m_2-m_3 = 0
    • or [g]=[a+d+f+g]−[a]−[d]−[f][g] = [a+d+f+g] - [a] - [d] - [f]   df(g)=m1−m1−0−0=0\text{df}(g) = m_1-m_1-0-0 = 0
    1. [h]=residuals=1−[a+b+c+d+e+f+g][h] = \text{residuals} = 1 - [a+b+c+d+e+f+g]   df2(h)=n−1−m7\text{df}_2(h) = n-1-m_7 (for denominator of FF)

Tests of Significance

  • F(a+d+f+g)=[a+d+f+g]/m1[b+c+e+h]/(n−1−m1)F(a+d+f+g) = \frac{[a+d+f+g]/m_1}{[b+c+e+h]/(n-1-m_1)}
  • F(b+d+e+g)=[b+d+e+g]/m2[a+c+f+h]/(n−1−m2)F(b+d+e+g) = \frac{[b+d+e+g]/m_2}{[a+c+f+h]/(n-1-m_2)}
  • F(c+e+f+g)=[c+e+f+g]/m3[a+b+d+h]/(n−1−m3)F(c+e+f+g) = \frac{[c+e+f+g]/m_3}{[a+b+d+h]/(n-1-m_3)}
  • F(a+b+d+e+f+g)=[a+b+d+e+f+g]/m4[c+h]/(n−1−m4)F(a+b+d+e+f+g) = \frac{[a+b+d+e+f+g]/m_4}{[c+h]/(n-1-m_4)}
  • F(a+c+d+e+f+g)=[a+c+d+e+f+g]/m5[b+h]/(n−1−m5)F(a+c+d+e+f+g) = \frac{[a+c+d+e+f+g]/m_5}{[b+h]/(n-1-m_5)}
  • F(b+c+d+e+f+g)=[b+c+d+e+f+g]/m6[a+h]/(n−1−m6)F(b+c+d+e+f+g) = \frac{[b+c+d+e+f+g]/m_6}{[a+h]/(n-1-m_6)}
  • F(a+b+c+d+e+f+g)=[a+b+c+d+e+f+g]/m7[h]/(n−1−m7)F(a+b+c+d+e+f+g) = \frac{[a+b+c+d+e+f+g]/m_7}{[h]/(n-1-m_7)}
  • F(a)=[a]/(m7−m6)[h]/(n−1−m7)F(a) = \frac{[a]/(m_7-m_6)}{[h]/(n-1-m_7)}
  • F(b)=[b]/(m7−m5)[h]/(n−1−m7)F(b) = \frac{[b]/(m_7-m_5)}{[h]/(n-1-m_7)}
  • F(c)=[c]/(m7−m4)[h]/(n−1−m7)F(c) = \frac{[c]/(m_7-m_4)}{[h]/(n-1-m_7)}
  • F(a+d)=[a+d]/(m5−m3)[b+h]/(n−1−m5)F(a+d) = \frac{[a+d]/(m_5-m_3)}{[b+h]/(n-1-m_5)}
  • F(a+f)=[a+f]/(m4−m2)[c+h]/(n−1−m4)F(a+f) = \frac{[a+f]/(m_4-m_2)}{[c+h]/(n-1-m_4)}
  • F(b+d)=[b+d]/(m6−m3)[a+h]/(n−1−m6)F(b+d) = \frac{[b+d]/(m_6-m_3)}{[a+h]/(n-1-m_6)}
  • F(b+e)=[b+e]/(m4−m1)[c+h]/(n−1−m4)F(b+e) = \frac{[b+e]/(m_4-m_1)}{[c+h]/(n-1-m_4)}
  • F(c+e)=[c+e]/(m5−m1)[b+h]/(n−1−m5)F(c+e) = \frac{[c+e]/(m_5-m_1)}{[b+h]/(n-1-m_5)}
  • F(c+f)=[c+f]/(m6−m2)[a+h]/(n−1−m6)F(c+f) = \frac{[c+f]/(m_6-m_2)}{[a+h]/(n-1-m_6)}

3. Four Explanatory Data Tables

  • Table 1 with m1m_1 variables, Table 2 with m2m_2 variables, Table 3 with m3m_3 variables, Table 4 with m4m_4 variables.
  • Number of fractions: 16, called [a][a] to [p][p].
  • ✓\checkmark indicates the 15 regression or canonical analyses that have to be computed.
Type Compute Fitted Residuals Derived fractions Degrees of freedom
✓\checkmark Y⋅1Y \cdot 1 [a+e+g+h+k+l+n+o][a+e+g+h+k+l+n+o] [b+c+d+f+i+j+m+p][b+c+d+f+i+j+m+p] (1) df=m1\text{df} = m_1
✓\checkmark Y⋅2Y \cdot 2 [b+e+f+i+k+l+m+o][b+e+f+i+k+l+m+o] [a+c+d+g+h+j+n+p][a+c+d+g+h+j+n+p] (2) df=m2\text{df} = m_2
✓\checkmark Y⋅3Y \cdot 3 [c+f+g+j+l+m+n+o][c+f+g+j+l+m+n+o] [a+b+d+e+h+i+k+p][a+b+d+e+h+i+k+p] (3) df=m3\text{df} = m_3
✓\checkmark Y⋅4Y \cdot 4 [d+h+i+j+k+m+n+o][d+h+i+j+k+m+n+o] [a+b+c+e+f+g+l+p][a+b+c+e+f+g+l+p] (4) df=m4\text{df} = m_4
✓\checkmark Y⋅1,2Y \cdot 1,2 [a+b+e+f+g+h+i+k+l+m+n+o][a+b+e+f+g+h+i+k+l+m+n+o] [c+d+j+p][c+d+j+p] (5) df=m5≤m1+m2\text{df} = m_5 \le m_1+m_2
✓\checkmark Y⋅1,3Y \cdot 1,3 [a+c+e+f+g+h+j+k+l+m+n+o][a+c+e+f+g+h+j+k+l+m+n+o] [b+d+i+p][b+d+i+p] (6) df=m6≤m1+m3\text{df} = m_6 \le m_1+m_3
✓\checkmark Y⋅1,4Y \cdot 1,4 [a+d+e+g+h+i+j+k+l+m+n+o][a+d+e+g+h+i+j+k+l+m+n+o] [b+c+f+p][b+c+f+p] (7) df=m7≤m1+m4\text{df} = m_7 \le m_1+m_4
✓\checkmark Y⋅2,3Y \cdot 2,3 [b+c+e+f+g+i+j+k+l+m+n+o][b+c+e+f+g+i+j+k+l+m+n+o] [a+d+h+p][a+d+h+p] (8) df=m8≤m2+m3\text{df} = m_8 \le m_2+m_3
✓\checkmark Y⋅2,4Y \cdot 2,4 [b+d+e+f+h+i+j+k+l+m+n+o][b+d+e+f+h+i+j+k+l+m+n+o] [a+c+g+p][a+c+g+p] (9) df=m9≤m2+m4\text{df} = m_9 \le m_2+m_4
✓\checkmark Y⋅3,4Y \cdot 3,4 [c+d+f+g+h+i+j+k+l+m+n+o][c+d+f+g+h+i+j+k+l+m+n+o] [a+b+e+p][a+b+e+p] (10) df=m10≤m3+m4\text{df} = m_{10} \le m_3+m_4
✓\checkmark Y⋅1,2,3Y \cdot 1,2,3 [a+b+c+e+f+g+h+i+j+k+l+m+n+o][a+b+c+e+f+g+h+i+j+k+l+m+n+o] [d+p][d+p] (11) df=m11≤m1+m2+m3\text{df} = m_{11} \le m_1+m_2+m_3
✓\checkmark Y⋅1,2,4Y \cdot 1,2,4 [a+b+d+e+f+g+h+i+j+k+l+m+n+o][a+b+d+e+f+g+h+i+j+k+l+m+n+o] [c+p][c+p] (12) df=m12≤m1+m2+m4\text{df} = m_{12} \le m_1+m_2+m_4
✓\checkmark Y⋅1,3,4Y \cdot 1,3,4 [a+c+d+e+f+g+h+i+j+k+l+m+n+o][a+c+d+e+f+g+h+i+j+k+l+m+n+o] [b+p][b+p] (13) df=m13≤m1+m3+m4\text{df} = m_{13} \le m_1+m_3+m_4
✓\checkmark Y⋅2,3,4Y \cdot 2,3,4 [b+c+d+e+f+g+h+i+j+k+l+m+n+o][b+c+d+e+f+g+h+i+j+k+l+m+n+o] [a+p][a+p] (14) df=m14≤m2+m3+m4\text{df} = m_{14} \le m_2+m_3+m_4
✓\checkmark Y⋅1,2,3,4Y \cdot 1,2,3,4 [a+b+c+d+e+f+g+h+i+j+k+l+m+n+o][a+b+c+d+e+f+g+h+i+j+k+l+m+n+o] [p][p] (15) df=m15≤m1+m2+m3+m4\text{df} = m_{15} \le m_1+m_2+m_3+m_4

Partial Analyses

  • Controlling for one table XX:
      1. [a+g+h+n]=[a+b+e+f+g+h+i+k+l+m+n+o]−[b+e+f+i+k+l+m+o][a+g+h+n] = [a+b+e+f+g+h+i+k+l+m+n+o] - [b+e+f+i+k+l+m+o]   df=m5−m2\text{df} = m_5 - m_2
      1. [a+e+h+k]=[a+c+e+f+g+h+j+k+l+m+n+o]−[c+f+g+j+l+m+n+o][a+e+h+k] = [a+c+e+f+g+h+j+k+l+m+n+o] - [c+f+g+j+l+m+n+o]   df=m6−m3\text{df} = m_6 - m_3
      1. [a+e+g+l]=[a+d+e+g+h+i+j+k+l+m+n+o]−[d+h+i+j+k+m+n+o][a+e+g+l] = [a+d+e+g+h+i+j+k+l+m+n+o] - [d+h+i+j+k+m+n+o]   df=m7−m4\text{df} = m_7 - m_4
      1. [b+f+i+m]=[a+b+e+f+g+h+i+k+l+m+n+o]−[a+e+g+h+k+l+n+o][b+f+i+m] = [a+b+e+f+g+h+i+k+l+m+n+o] - [a+e+g+h+k+l+n+o]   df=m5−m1\text{df} = m_5 - m_1
      1. [b+e+i+k]=[b+c+e+f+g+i+j+k+l+m+n+o]−[c+f+g+j+l+m+n+o][b+e+i+k] = [b+c+e+f+g+i+j+k+l+m+n+o] - [c+f+g+j+l+m+n+o]   df=m8−m3\text{df} = m_8 - m_3
      1. [b+e+f+l]=[b+d+e+f+h+i+j+k+l+m+n+o]−[d+h+i+j+k+m+n+o][b+e+f+l] = [b+d+e+f+h+i+j+k+l+m+n+o] - [d+h+i+j+k+m+n+o]   df=m9−m4\text{df} = m_9 - m_4
      1. [c+f+j+m]=[a+c+e+f+g+h+j+k+l+m+n+o]−[a+e+g+h+k+l+n+o][c+f+j+m] = [a+c+e+f+g+h+j+k+l+m+n+o] - [a+e+g+h+k+l+n+o]   df=m6−m1\text{df} = m_6 - m_1
      1. [c+g+j+n]=[b+c+e+f+g+i+j+k+l+m+n+o]−[b+e+f+i+k+l+m+o][c+g+j+n] = [b+c+e+f+g+i+j+k+l+m+n+o] - [b+e+f+i+k+l+m+o]   df=m8−m2\text{df} = m_8 - m_2
      1. [c+f+g+l]=[c+d+f+g+h+i+j+k+l+m+n+o]−[d+h+i+j+k+m+n+o][c+f+g+l] = [c+d+f+g+h+i+j+k+l+m+n+o] - [d+h+i+j+k+m+n+o]   df=m10−m4\text{df} = m_{10} - m_4
      1. [d+i+j+m]=[a+d+e+g+h+i+j+k+l+m+n+o]−[a+e+g+h+k+l+n+o][d+i+j+m] = [a+d+e+g+h+i+j+k+l+m+n+o] - [a+e+g+h+k+l+n+o]   df=m7−m1\text{df} = m_7 - m_1
      1. [d+h+j+n]=[b+d+e+f+h+i+j+k+l+m+n+o]−[b+e+f+i+k+l+m+o][d+h+j+n] = [b+d+e+f+h+i+j+k+l+m+n+o] - [b+e+f+i+k+l+m+o]   df=m9−m2\text{df} = m_9 - m_2
      1. [d+h+i+k]=[c+d+f+g+h+i+j+k+l+m+n+o]−[c+f+g+j+l+m+n+o][d+h+i+k] = [c+d+f+g+h+i+j+k+l+m+n+o] - [c+f+g+j+l+m+n+o]   df=m10−m3\text{df} = m_{10} - m_3
  • Controlling for two tables XX:
      1. [a+e]=[a+c+d+e+f+g+h+i+j+k+l+m+n+o]−[c+d+f+g+h+i+j+k+l+m+n+o][a+e] = [a+c+d+e+f+g+h+i+j+k+l+m+n+o] - [c+d+f+g+h+i+j+k+l+m+n+o]   df=m13−m10\text{df} = m_{13} - m_{10}
      1. [a+g]=[a+b+d+e+f+g+h+i+j+k+l+m+n+o]−[b+d+e+f+h+i+j+k+l+m+n+o][a+g] = [a+b+d+e+f+g+h+i+j+k+l+m+n+o] - [b+d+e+f+h+i+j+k+l+m+n+o]   df=m12−m9\text{df} = m_{12} - m_9
      1. [a+h]=[a+b+c+e+f+g+h+i+j+k+l+m+n+o]−[b+c+e+f+g+i+j+k+l+m+n+o][a+h] = [a+b+c+e+f+g+h+i+j+k+l+m+n+o] - [b+c+e+f+g+i+j+k+l+m+n+o]   df=m11−m8\text{df} = m_{11} - m_8
      1. [b+e]=[b+c+d+e+f+g+h+i+j+k+l+m+n+o]−[c+d+f+g+h+i+j+k+l+m+n+o][b+e] = [b+c+d+e+f+g+h+i+j+k+l+m+n+o] - [c+d+f+g+h+i+j+k+l+m+n+o]   df=m14−m10\text{df} = m_{14} - m_{10}
      1. [b+f]=[a+b+d+e+f+g+h+i+j+k+l+m+n+o]−[a+d+e+g+h+i+j+k+l+m+n+o][b+f] = [a+b+d+e+f+g+h+i+j+k+l+m+n+o] - [a+d+e+g+h+i+j+k+l+m+n+o]   df=m12−m7\text{df} = m_{12} - m_7
      1. [b+i]=[a+b+c+e+f+g+h+i+j+k+l+m+n+o]−[a+c+e+f+g+h+j+k+l+m+n+o][b+i] = [a+b+c+e+f+g+h+i+j+k+l+m+n+o] - [a+c+e+f+g+h+j+k+l+m+n+o]   df=m11−m6\text{df} = m_{11} - m_6
      1. [c+f]=[a+c+d+e+f+g+h+i+j+k+l+m+n+o]−[a+d+e+g+h+i+j+k+l+m+n+o][c+f] = [a+c+d+e+f+g+h+i+j+k+l+m+n+o] - [a+d+e+g+h+i+j+k+l+m+n+o]   df=m13−m7\text{df} = m_{13} - m_7
      1. [c+g]=[b+c+d+e+f+g+h+i+j+k+l+m+n+o]−[b+d+e+f+h+i+j+k+l+m+n+o][c+g] = [b+c+d+e+f+g+h+i+j+k+l+m+n+o] - [b+d+e+f+h+i+j+k+l+m+n+o]   df=m14−m9\text{df} = m_{14} - m_9
      1. [c+j]=[a+b+c+e+f+g+h+i+j+k+l+m+n+o]−[a+b+e+f+g+h+i+k+l+m+n+o][c+j] = [a+b+c+e+f+g+h+i+j+k+l+m+n+o] - [a+b+e+f+g+h+i+k+l+m+n+o]   df=m11−m5\text{df} = m_{11} - m_5
      1. [d+h]=[b+c+d+e+f+g+h+i+j+k+l+m+n+o]−[b+c+e+f+g+i+j+k+l+m+n+o][d+h] = [b+c+d+e+f+g+h+i+j+k+l+m+n+o] - [b+c+e+f+g+i+j+k+l+m+n+o]   df=m14−m8\text{df} = m_{14} - m_8
      1. [d+i]=[a+c+d+e+f+g+h+i+j+k+l+m+n+o]−[a+c+e+f+g+h+j+k+l+m+n+o][d+i] = [a+c+d+e+f+g+h+i+j+k+l+m+n+o] - [a+c+e+f+g+h+j+k+l+m+n+o]   df=m13−m6\text{df} = m_{13} - m_6
      1. [d+j]=[a+b+d+e+f+g+h+i+j+k+l+m+n+o]−[a+b+e+f+g+h+i+k+l+m+n+o][d+j] = [a+b+d+e+f+g+h+i+j+k+l+m+n+o] - [a+b+e+f+g+h+i+k+l+m+n+o]   df=m12−m5\text{df} = m_{12} - m_5
  • Controlling for three tables XX:
      1. [a]=[a+b+c+d+e+f+g+h+i+j+k+l+m+n+o]−[b+c+d+e+f+g+h+i+j+k+l+m+n+o][a] = [a+b+c+d+e+f+g+h+i+j+k+l+m+n+o] - [b+c+d+e+f+g+h+i+j+k+l+m+n+o]   df=m15−m14\text{df} = m_{15} - m_{14}
      1. [b]=[a+b+c+d+e+f+g+h+i+j+k+l+m+n+o]−[a+c+d+e+f+g+h+i+j+k+l+m+n+o][b] = [a+b+c+d+e+f+g+h+i+j+k+l+m+n+o] - [a+c+d+e+f+g+h+i+j+k+l+m+n+o]   df=m15−m13\text{df} = m_{15} - m_{13}
      1. [c]=[a+b+c+d+e+f+g+h+i+j+k+l+m+n+o]−[a+b+d+e+f+g+h+i+j+k+l+m+n+o][c] = [a+b+c+d+e+f+g+h+i+j+k+l+m+n+o] - [a+b+d+e+f+g+h+i+j+k+l+m+n+o]   df=m15−m12\text{df} = m_{15} - m_{12}
      1. [d]=[a+b+c+d+e+f+g+h+i+j+k+l+m+n+o]−[a+b+c+e+f+g+h+i+j+k+l+m+n+o][d] = [a+b+c+d+e+f+g+h+i+j+k+l+m+n+o] - [a+b+c+e+f+g+h+i+j+k+l+m+n+o]   df=m15−m11\text{df} = m_{15} - m_{11}

Fractions Estimated by Subtraction (cannot be tested)

    1. [e]=[a+e]−[a][e] = [a+e] - [a]   df=m1−m1=0\text{df} = m_1-m_1 = 0
    1. [f]=[b+f]−[b][f] = [b+f] - [b]   df=m2−m2=0\text{df} = m_2-m_2 = 0
    1. [g]=[a+g]−[a][g] = [a+g] - [a]   df=m1−m1=0\text{df} = m_1-m_1 = 0
    1. [h]=[a+h]−[a][h] = [a+h] - [a]   df=m1−m1=0\text{df} = m_1-m_1 = 0
    1. [i]=[b+i]−[b][i] = [b+i] - [b]   df=m2−m2=0\text{df} = m_2-m_2 = 0
    1. [j]=[c+j]−[c][j] = [c+j] - [c]   df=m3−m3=0\text{df} = m_3-m_3 = 0
    1. [k]=[a+e+h+k]−[a+e]−[h][k] = [a+e+h+k] - [a+e] - [h]   df=m1−m1−0=0\text{df} = m_1-m_1-0 = 0
    1. [l]=[a+e+g+l]−[a+e]−[g][l] = [a+e+g+l] - [a+e] - [g]   df=m1−m1−0=0\text{df} = m_1-m_1-0 = 0
    1. [m]=[b+f+i+m]−[b+f]−[i][m] = [b+f+i+m] - [b+f] - [i]   df=m2−m2−0=0\text{df} = m_2-m_2-0 = 0
    1. [n]=[a+g+h+n]−[a+g]−[h][n] = [a+g+h+n] - [a+g] - [h]   df=m1−m1−0=0\text{df} = m_1-m_1-0 = 0
    1. [o]=[a+e+g+h+k+l+n+o]−[a+e+h+k]−[g]−[l]−[n][o] = [a+e+g+h+k+l+n+o] - [a+e+h+k] - [g] - [l] - [n]   df=m1−m1−0−0−0=0\text{df} = m_1-m_1-0-0-0 = 0
    1. [p]=residuals=1−[a+b+c+d+e+f+g+h+i+j+k+l+m+n+o][p] = \text{residuals} = 1 - [a+b+c+d+e+f+g+h+i+j+k+l+m+n+o]   df2(p)=n−1−m15\text{df}_2(p) = n-1-m_{15}

Tests of Significance

Tests of significance for the fractions and combinations of fractions are constructed in the same way as for two and three tables: * F(a+e+g+h+k+l+n+o)=[a+e+g+h+k+l+n+o]/m1[b+c+d+f+i+j+m+p]/(n−1−m1)F(a+e+g+h+k+l+n+o) = \frac{[a+e+g+h+k+l+n+o]/m_1}{[b+c+d+f+i+j+m+p]/(n-1-m_1)} * F(b+e+f+i+k+l+m+o)=[b+e+f+i+k+l+m+o]/m2[a+c+d+g+h+j+n+p]/(n−1−m2)F(b+e+f+i+k+l+m+o) = \frac{[b+e+f+i+k+l+m+o]/m_2}{[a+c+d+g+h+j+n+p]/(n-1-m_2)} * F(c+f+g+j+l+m+n+o)=[c+f+g+j+l+m+n+o]/m3[a+b+d+e+h+i+k+p]/(n−1−m3)F(c+f+g+j+l+m+n+o) = \frac{[c+f+g+j+l+m+n+o]/m_3}{[a+b+d+e+h+i+k+p]/(n-1-m_3)} * F(d+h+i+j+k+m+n+o)=[d+h+i+j+k+m+n+o]/m4[a+b+c+e+f+g+l+p]/(n−1−m4)F(d+h+i+j+k+m+n+o) = \frac{[d+h+i+j+k+m+n+o]/m_4}{[a+b+c+e+f+g+l+p]/(n-1-m_4)} * F(a+b+e+f+g+h+i+k+l+m+n+o)=[a+b+e+f+g+h+i+k+l+m+n+o]/m5[c+d+j+p]/(n−1−m5)F(a+b+e+f+g+h+i+k+l+m+n+o) = \frac{[a+b+e+f+g+h+i+k+l+m+n+o]/m_5}{[c+d+j+p]/(n-1-m_5)} * F(a+c+e+f+g+h+j+k+l+m+n+o)=[a+c+e+f+g+h+j+k+l+m+n+o]/m6[b+d+i+p]/(n−1−m6)F(a+c+e+f+g+h+j+k+l+m+n+o) = \frac{[a+c+e+f+g+h+j+k+l+m+n+o]/m_6}{[b+d+i+p]/(n-1-m_6)} * F(a+d+e+g+h+i+j+k+l+m+n+o)=[a+d+e+g+h+i+j+k+l+m+n+o]/m7[b+c+f+p]/(n−1−m7)F(a+d+e+g+h+i+j+k+l+m+n+o) = \frac{[a+d+e+g+h+i+j+k+l+m+n+o]/m_7}{[b+c+f+p]/(n-1-m_7)} * F(b+c+e+f+g+i+j+k+l+m+n+o)=[b+c+e+f+g+i+j+k+l+m+n+o]/m8[a+d+h+p]/(n−1−m8)F(b+c+e+f+g+i+j+k+l+m+n+o) = \frac{[b+c+e+f+g+i+j+k+l+m+n+o]/m_8}{[a+d+h+p]/(n-1-m_8)} * F(b+d+e+f+h+i+j+k+l+m+n+o)=[b+d+e+f+h+i+j+k+l+m+n+o]/m9[a+c+g+p]/(n−1−m9)F(b+d+e+f+h+i+j+k+l+m+n+o) = \frac{[b+d+e+f+h+i+j+k+l+m+n+o]/m_9}{[a+c+g+p]/(n-1-m_9)} * F(c+d+f+g+h+i+j+k+l+m+n+o)=[c+d+f+g+h+i+j+k+l+m+n+o]/m10[a+b+e+p]/(n−1−m10)F(c+d+f+g+h+i+j+k+l+m+n+o) = \frac{[c+d+f+g+h+i+j+k+l+m+n+o]/m_{10}}{[a+b+e+p]/(n-1-m_{10})} * F(a+b+c+e+f+g+h+i+j+k+l+m+n+o)=[a+b+c+e+f+g+h+i+j+k+l+m+n+o]/m11[d+p]/(n−1−m11)F(a+b+c+e+f+g+h+i+j+k+l+m+n+o) = \frac{[a+b+c+e+f+g+h+i+j+k+l+m+n+o]/m_{11}}{[d+p]/(n-1-m_{11})} * F(a+b+d+e+f+g+h+i+j+k+l+m+n+o)=[a+b+d+e+f+g+h+i+j+k+l+m+n+o]/m12[c+p]/(n−1−m12)F(a+b+d+e+f+g+h+i+j+k+l+m+n+o) = \frac{[a+b+d+e+f+g+h+i+j+k+l+m+n+o]/m_{12}}{[c+p]/(n-1-m_{12})} * F(a+c+d+e+f+g+h+i+j+k+l+m+n+o)=[a+c+d+e+f+g+h+i+j+k+l+m+n+o]/m13[b+p]/(n−1−m13)F(a+c+d+e+f+g+h+i+j+k+l+m+n+o) = \frac{[a+c+d+e+f+g+h+i+j+k+l+m+n+o]/m_{13}}{[b+p]/(n-1-m_{13})} * F(b+c+d+e+f+g+h+i+j+k+l+m+n+o)=[b+c+d+e+f+g+h+i+j+k+l+m+n+o]/m14[a+p]/(n−1−m14)F(b+c+d+e+f+g+h+i+j+k+l+m+n+o) = \frac{[b+c+d+e+f+g+h+i+j+k+l+m+n+o]/m_{14}}{[a+p]/(n-1-m_{14})} * F(a+b+c+d+e+f+g+h+i+j+k+l+m+n+o)=[a+b+c+d+e+f+g+h+i+j+k+l+m+n+o]/m15[p]/(n−1−m15)F(a+b+c+d+e+f+g+h+i+j+k+l+m+n+o) = \frac{[a+b+c+d+e+f+g+h+i+j+k+l+m+n+o]/m_{15}}{[p]/(n-1-m_{15})}

  • For the individual fractions controlling for all other tables:
    • F(a)=[a]/(m15−m14)[p]/(n−1−m15)F(a) = \frac{[a]/(m_{15}-m_{14})}{[p]/(n-1-m_{15})}
    • F(b)=[b]/(m15−m13)[p]/(n−1−m15)F(b) = \frac{[b]/(m_{15}-m_{13})}{[p]/(n-1-m_{15})}
    • F(c)=[c]/(m15−m12)[p]/(n−1−m15)F(c) = \frac{[c]/(m_{15}-m_{12})}{[p]/(n-1-m_{15})}
    • F(d)=[d]/(m15−m11)[p]/(n−1−m15)F(d) = \frac{[d]/(m_{15}-m_{11})}{[p]/(n-1-m_{15})}
  • For individual fractions controlling for one table XX:
    • F(a+g+h+n)=[a+g+h+n]/(m5−m2)[c+d+j+p]/(n−1−m5)F(a+g+h+n) = \frac{[a+g+h+n]/(m_5-m_2)}{[c+d+j+p]/(n-1-m_5)}
    • (And similarly for other fractions controlling for one table XX)
  • For individual fractions controlling for two tables XX:
    • F(a+e)=[a+e]/(m13−m10)[b+p]/(n−1−m13)F(a+e) = \frac{[a+e]/(m_{13}-m_{10})}{[b+p]/(n-1-m_{13})}
    • (And similarly for other fractions controlling for two tables XX)
  • Fractions controlling for three tables XX:
    • F(a)=[a]/(m15−m14)[p]/(n−1−m15)F(a) = \frac{[a]/(m_{15}-m_{14})}{[p]/(n-1-m_{15})}
    • F(b)=[b]/(m15−m13)[p]/(n−1−m15)F(b) = \frac{[b]/(m_{15}-m_{13})}{[p]/(n-1-m_{15})}
    • F(c)=[c]/(m15−m12)[p]/(n−1−m15)F(c) = \frac{[c]/(m_{15}-m_{12})}{[p]/(n-1-m_{15})}
    • F(d)=[d]/(m15−m11)[p]/(n−1−m15)F(d) = \frac{[d]/(m_{15}-m_{11})}{[p]/(n-1-m_{15})}

[!IMPORTANT] Other fractions combining elementary fractions [a][a] to [o][o] can be calculated, but cannot be tested because they cannot be obtained by regression or canonical analysis.

On this page

Developed by Paul E. Santos Andrade, Jari Oksanen, Gavin L. Simpson, F. Guillaume Blanchet, Roeland Kindt, Pierre Legendre, Peter R. Minchin, R.B. O’Hara, Peter Solymos, M. Henry H. Stevens, Eduard Szoecs, Helene Wagner, Michael Bedward, Ben Bolker, Daniel Borcard, Gustavo Carvalho, Miquel De Caceres, Sebastien Durand, Heloisa Beatriz Antoniazi Evangelista, Geoffrey Hannigan, Mark O. Hill, Leo Lahti, Cameron Martino, Marie-Helene Ouellette, Eduardo Ribeiro Cunha, Tyler Smith, Adrian Stier, Cajo J.F. Ter Braak, James Weedon.

Site built with pkgdown 2.2.0.
